textByHash.000e67e5d51d83f01f182252543a60f653784eb9: Two people who start out hating each other, and end up falling in love. It's a classic tale.
textByHash.0953dc5bcdfa39aecb5ab408dc2c9a080938fee2: You're right, I am not interested in more responsibilities. I would have less time for poetry! Is that your final decision?
textByHash.099e2b58faf6aa2638e93e32d3b46f8a516b90ae: I composed a poem, would you like to hear it?
textByHash.0aea1eb40c10c2f736d073912d2383a9fc9fe4d9: I'm sorry you feel that way.
textByHash.0af713f7cc6a2ded1a0e13e0c888680cb7654078: 'It's somewhere in the sea, And it looks very nice, If it weren't for your ancestors, It wouldn't be there at all,'
textByHash.0cebb2598c9ae88e40296732d0cce02cbb4feee7: I think you'll make a great Thane for our kingdom.
textByHash.0e8f2027e4bb9d68146f11cf6af2ce158f181265: You've already given me some, but thank you.
textByHash.104f506563d96b3060b5d7050182172e33e27bc2: Well, if my father had had something to occupy him, he wouldn't have gotten bored and decided to argue with Queen Sigrid again.
textByHash.12485ffd75213e4b90a2a4d6ec7f8c6a7272206b: [Then I shall I call you , sir/Then I shall I call you , madam/Then I shall I call you , and you shall call me Prince.
textByHash.12788e9140dccd8009af90e92ce5a52ad5c2cd73: Have you managed to make peace between my father and Queen Sigrid yet?
textByHash.141d8de6f184f6b0ebb137308b321128ddc41691: Quiet! What would you know about poetry?
textByHash.14d987fc78060e2dc5d1745f29ef9d159f23168c: I'm sure you can manage to arrange it all.
textByHash.172f4d2acbd6637f23ab3f4255da3aedbcc59313: Mmm, a very good answer, madam.
textByHash.18318f6f7bff5a2d98c703ab8f94833041fcccab: Music and poetry. Surely you haven't forgotten already?
textByHash.18c96b4214ba4b9b331d2a41714bd0ade279a3be: Good day. Have you come to see my sister?
textByHash.193ba1fd48d4a1f22d7df44a97f63fae960264cf: Fine! See if I write you any more songs!
textByHash.1c38c2f63bd32bd59cef51165091af60550cde09: Our national anthems are wonderful, won't you sing this song?'
textByHash.1ee91046ca36ad94420db05647577422ca5ee038: Perhaps it's an odd idea, my dear, but since we don't have an army, we can hardly declare war...
textByHash.245c8ff8a08933fcd7c55ddf51426f0116964958: And he became the world's greatest bard, And delighted one and all, With his songs and sagas, And Brand was his name!
textByHash.28f84f3bc10910504a430332e7689a042843e9aa: And why do you think that might be, sir?
textByHash.29003941c717351545b2c273ad73faeb2b299f0a: You're right, it takes great skill and inspiration to be a great poet such as myself.
textByHash.2ba3b51053538532121bdfad223dbd3d1e83acbb: Don't lower the art of poetry with such bawdy rhymes!
textByHash.2cec5d2bf70b63b5154f0d7cfbf8410e5be9f053: 'Etceteria, Etceteria, Oh what a great country it is, Etceteria, Etceteria, So I wrote a song about it.'
textByHash.2f25e5d4c43ac4e0242f2eacd06bf8c3a2328f32: Your hair is like a waterfall Or perhaps a bramble bush Your eyes are like an ocean on which I want to sail the ship of our connection!
textByHash.3160c49f3ef9f34b2f00ae535ef05c075eb811c4: Come now, what interest would I have in a crass adventurer like yourself? What would you know of the poetry that flows through my veins like an avalanche through a peaceful mountain village?
textByHash.33942a570fcd00453ea368085c8b99a8a67988cc: We don't always see eye-to-eye, but she understands my art better than our father.
textByHash.340a8ecfdb35fa7a817061c50b52476d75bcf518: Oh, my darling, you've made me the happiest person alive.
textByHash.346cc8e60ab438e8aca21f9da3fa79fdebfc6b1b: I'm glad you liked it, my dear.
textByHash.36b7f73da308299b10ff0233ad9d376b48c30928: Fear not! I made many, many more!
textByHash.397b67bba454b17f4a2070d4698d6e0ee80b868f: Thank you! I thought I would compose something for future generations, to tell them my story.
textByHash.3a13665c23f44f708f9f04211f6be2dca3c60ed5: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done, [dear/.
textByHash.3c1b5417b7cd27219bbeba332a7d44a4a1812181: Let's not get ahead of ourselves now!
textByHash.40464bac1878819c6f55fb141755395117ab1627: How are you doing, dear? Have you convinced my father yet?
textByHash.416a406ebf420c4bcd3688d951e0e24613ab7404: I'm afraid I haven't managed to find a priest for our marriage yet.
textByHash.43a6cd68aeadc4938d0389c25d3567199de7492b: Oh, but I realise you have not yet told me your name!
textByHash.47ac3e1c0f7f50d32e7d07216cd2087406e3fc54: It's a short one, I'll admit...
textByHash.4b1c70831b2b8f8b1873291ebf5b898a36c9efb6: And why do you think that might be?
textByHash.4d6b7cffd00270a843918fcc947c2316881b3de4: Why thank you. Sometimes I amaze even myself.
textByHash.4dbc2cace6196371e1f5e2adf1f0502138454b7c: What a beautiful gift! I think you'd make a great Thane running our realm. Can you inform my father that you have earned my trust?
textByHash.50ba34bd2a442d26b3e6f6ffe35bfa4c09a49fa4: Let's not get ahead of ourselves now!
textByHash.56efd3e95c0b35b47d74e491f4ddabeecf7504bf: Hm, no, I don't think that's the right reason.
textByHash.5aad490d356686a01057637b9c5a4484d2228702: Tell me, have you ever composed any poems yourself?
textByHash.5c0a6cf56c580c962c28880653cd8da65264b453: I'll ask Father if he minds us discussing this in the throne room for a while.
textByHash.5dedd2964fc97e2fe856523b2cc42053ac6e320f: Yes! We'll have to put off the actual ceremony until we can find a priest, but you can tell my father I have agreed to marry you.
textByHash.5f7280f977053a45b6817aa989dd3637b0a49f7f: Let's not get ahead of ourselves now, sir!
textByHash.60020c3ab8266effdaa9d8b4c1e2717bcfb5da68: My heart is an open book to you, my dear, and all the pages say yes... ''Yes''!
textByHash.6179690eef58d756afd8584ead6302c40b067ade: Of course it will! It always happens in the ballads and stories.
textByHash.7395a06b9c71dfeba52828068872e24894fb5a89: Yes! We'll have to put off the actual ceremony until we can find a priest, but I will tell my father I have agreed to marry you.
textByHash.751bcb8d277c0ca1f4e310a41b62be9e2e5693d0: I wrote a song about you. Would you like to hear it?
textByHash.784b58d99bad733dd81bb0da037c3529775666f7: Let's not get ahead of ourselves now, madam!
textByHash.7cc2f7c3dbc11cb8f1950df2f6dbde38910d3dba: 'And when things aren't going too well, We sing this lovely song, And then we'll all feel so much better, It won't take very long.'
textByHash.7d04ac007d3f5f405a3effed525edf8d0a1124de: Oh, my dear, you've made me the happiest person alive.
textByHash.7df4344529d78229bec6bb9d5df11f0cd4b8b975: Thank you. It's nice to be appreciated.
textByHash.7e4b0708c8c0dfee3681e7205d35e5c25ccc92ec: Poetry and music have always fascinated me. I am drawn to them like a moth to an overenthusiastic mage who has set himself on fire.
textByHash.7fc284fa11ac036d7181c6e76955813bbd1959fb: No problem, I'm already done! Here, listen!
textByHash.843259cec587cf2c211bb15f57904759c90904fe: Oh, I would never concern myself with such trivial matters as those involved in ruling a Kingdom. My interests lie solely in the arts.
textByHash.8a601f06d80115d43138ad127389f11358a420f4: Think of the poetic irony of the situation!
textByHash.8eb48ac0ba6f1059f56083c42ffedfa76172c832: And why do you think that might be, madam?
textByHash.918a69727021776fd6cc20212b02de05ffea15e1: It's terrible, my dear! My father's been arguing with the Queen of Etceteria, and has managed to start a war again.
textByHash.9227e4e8e10a28a5344852d6289e25212a262065: Yes; I play the lyre and the harp, and compose songs, poems and sagas to move you, to delight you and entertain you! Sadly, my father does not think much of the art. What do you think?
textByHash.92a3d50cbbab4ee60fede283b9ae951789514201: You've already given me one, but thank you.
textByHash.94a712719c3c3a7f0037c7fe0cd08d1619ca3bb3: Sorry, sir, but I'm not interested.
textByHash.9b0f992cae21f33bb6c71e83dc90ad40c28119bd: You don't seem to understand poetry at all...
textByHash.9d1f30034522c2a653bf8c5e6cef05d3a7536648: Of course, she couldn't understand as well as you do, my dear.
textByHash.9e3d866f252a09f73aeecd626e9177b1d791350c: Not quite yet, my good friend; let us just say, the future is a maze of inescapable decisions, and you are one possible answer.
textByHash.9fde69dc01e4da50fe60c289eeda25e9f89123f1: Sorry, madam, but I'm not interested.
textByHash.a118b244becc90d858d8ec417f5f8160628c8d79: Once upon a time there was a Prince, And Brand was his name, He always had a song in his heart, And a rhyme in his head,
textByHash.a684dcf2d584ed0d94739b97f71c81ca54d7e93d: 'Today is a happy day, we are getting married,
textByHash.a9e4ef6998a5d7a6834f6cfecbaff41a7461ebc9: This may be the case. I am willing to give you a chance; if you impress me enough, I will consider trusting you as our regent.
textByHash.aca02e25bf06027f8c0d3670a7a2134040ab4363: Was it really that hard to understand?
textByHash.adb5ed5fbebee562ae8f48d04b37d08e3b2bf55c: You have perhaps more poetry in your soul than I first thought, madam...
textByHash.aec3dd4d16e22e31e01026578409cadf2fdedee7: You've already given me one, remember?
textByHash.b05a24f6c87d1c22812984d49f389d96e8980dfe: I think we should persuade our father and Queen Sigrid to marry!
textByHash.b25878c0116d4b3d798b43252f3b21ca4123ce86: You have perhaps more poetry in your soul than I first thought, sir...
textByHash.b7dc64e133bb343f8bd1d20f306b09bc3274d3be: Well, I think you'll make a great Thane for our kingdom.
textByHash.b9fdeb2742da24b0fa3e277b7d56481e8f4035fb: I must admit I am growing quite fond of you.
textByHash.bb78b8384d6b397a6e49ac6de90a445cccdae52e: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done, darling.
textByHash.c6a18aefbae43dfb0dbe02e06c683812e0071442: I got up This morning, And had an egg For breakfast
textByHash.c868a345467c00a65802dcf4ba1e2d1f6528bb16: Do not fear, though; I shall call for you as soon as one is found.
textByHash.cd42da8a55e3683cf12a9b2b825b4a8277a765e6: So, will you talk to our father and Queen Sigrid, then?
textByHash.d02ed530686a44e4a5cc27c720026d15a8d5e104: I'm sure you'll manage to do it, dear.
textByHash.d3c931e58904dbe296d610724f20df1c58230107: If you don't appreciate my singing, you are free to leave!
textByHash.d48ffda4bb3c6bcc71628745c3621afe89024558: Here's a copy, you can keep it!
textByHash.d5a0bbfe6d0afec61d131d5f5b10bb505f9602d6: It is a simile, but what do you know of poetry?
textByHash.d771e56217ff880754e7839b00888c22160e9d26: Mmm, a very good answer, sir.
textByHash.d7a99b1c649e464cf0e2bfac793e7a76f547148d: It's awful that your work has been undone by such an accident.
textByHash.e0cee610eae2eed719158f9f3b8593efc735e99d: You have perhaps more poetry in your soul than I first thought...
textByHash.e80467066aacb4047f72558916f98cea0778a240: We'll have to put off the actual ceremony until we can find a priest, but you can tell my father I have agreed to marry you.
textByHash.eab403191c1cbf02aebfdd7e064ed27c26a4ef2d: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done.
textByHash.ebbd7cb487404d99f078eeb9134fc0a34b8b6aed: Astrid, I have an even better idea.
textByHash.ece4bfe612153dab1ff73c1a891ecfd6b9bbdab2: I'm afraid not, darling. I don't think there is one to be had on these islands for love nor money...
textByHash.ed1ce10f2d16d829a39a1140699c145b724b66ab: I composed a saga, would you like to hear it?
textByHash.f116ca3922fbcf1923d0d4a9095d917705068ec1: Don't worry, it's quite short. Here we go!
textByHash.fafd4d262ba27b7c66142bbc62c66ba425ccb011: You've come to the right bard! I'll write one for you immediately!
textByHash.fb0053f15639ec343e298f4c2ba2c81ecb00eaff: I'm afraid not, dear. I don't think there is one to be had on these islands for love nor money...
textByHash.fcb495f3aeeedee0d04e976776fdbedb110b1c4d: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done, dear friend.
textByHash.fcf211aa130ea301438844e98f7ab422e9282b66: The best part is that I could write their wedding hymn for them.
